# Customer Promise

## Business OS Promise

Every decision we make must protect this promise.

## We Promise

Your business data belongs to you.

Your time is valuable.

Your money deserves accurate calculations.

Your employees deserve fair payroll.

Your taxes should feel understandable.

Your software should work for you, not the other way around.

## We Will Never

- Never make software intentionally complicated.
- Never lock simple features behind unnecessary complexity.
- Never hide important business information.
- Never invent financial calculations.
- Never encourage decisions without explaining why.
- Never build features simply because competitors have them.

## We Will Always

- Always explain.
- Always verify.
- Always improve.
- Always listen.
- Always respect the owner's time.
- Always design for real businesses.
- Always make complicated things feel simple.

## Every Release Must Improve At Least One Of These

- Save time.
- Reduce mistakes.
- Reduce stress.
- Increase confidence.
- Improve business visibility.

If a release does not improve one of these, it should not be shipped.

## Our Definition of Success

A restaurant owner closes the shop.

A coffee shop owner finishes accounting.

A food vendor completes payroll.

A warehouse manager checks inventory.

They all smile because today's work finished faster than yesterday.

That is success.

## Final Promise

We are not building software.

We are building peace of mind for business owners.
